text:
This study aims to analyze the impact of a national Movement for Teacher Professional Development program namely “Merdeka Mengajar “(Independence in Teaching) for teacher professional development and technology use in vocational education context at SMK Negeri 1 Dlanggu Mojokerto. Based on qualitative case study, this research aims to investigate how this movement promotes educational quality through enhancing teacher’s pedagogical skills, building of collaborative learning environment and digital resources. The results show that Merdeka Mengajar programme enabled teachers to have easy access to on-line teaching resources and thus to modernize their teaching practices into more dynamic, creative and global market responsive skills which are required by the new work force in the global era.

While the move to online learning is in full swing, many barriers remain and the study uncovered many of them, particularly related to the technological barrier of getting new digital learning materials in sync with old curricula and the inadequacy of school infrastructure. Despite these challenges, teachers described the online learning platform as “very interactive” and that it was “very helpful in getting students involved in the learning process”. The study concludes that the teachers need training that is on-going to make full use of the digital learning platform and that there is a need for a more collaborative approach from Ministry of Education and policy makers towards teachers to implement the digital learning platform to the fullest potential of preparing students with the right vocational skills that are relevant and future-proof.
